description: OpenAI Codex usage — sessions, tokens, latency, tools, and decisions. Covers both the Codex CLI (codex_exec) and the Codex desktop app (codex-app-server).
title: Codex

options.content:
### 🤖 About this dashboard
Monitors **OpenAI Codex** activity across both surfaces: the **Codex CLI** (`service_name = codex_exec`) and the **Codex desktop app** (`service_name = codex-app-server`). All data is **log-sourced** from Codex's native OpenTelemetry export. Codex uses a WebSocket/SSE transport — latency comes from `codex.websocket_request` / `codex.websocket_event` durations, and **token counts** come from `codex.sse_event`. There is no per-request dollar cost in the Codex telemetry, so this dashboard tracks **usage and performance**, not spend.

panels.title: Input Tokens
panels.description: Total input (prompt) tokens across all turns.

panels.title: Output Tokens
panels.description: Total output (generated) tokens across all turns.

panels.title: Cached Tokens
panels.description: Total cached input tokens reused across turns (codex.sse_event cached_token_count). Higher cached share means cheaper, faster prompts.

panels.title: Reasoning Tokens
panels.description: Total reasoning tokens consumed (codex.sse_event reasoning_token_count) — the model's internal thinking budget.

panels.title: Total Tokens
panels.description: Sum of input + output tokens across all turns (codex.sse_event input_token_count + output_token_count). Cached and reasoning tokens are shown separately in the Tokens section.

targets.expr: sum(sum_over_time({service_name=~"codex-app-server|codex_exec"} | json | attributes_event_name="codex.sse_event" | unwrap attributes_input_token_count [$__range])) + sum(sum_over_time({service_name=~"codex-app-server|codex_exec"} | json | attributes_event_name="codex.sse_event" | unwrap attributes_output_token_count [$__range]))

panels.title: Total Turns (SSE completions)
panels.description: Count of completed model responses (codex.sse_event with event.kind = response.completed). A proxy for total request turns.

panels.title: Total Conversations
panels.description: Distinct Codex conversations started in the selected range (codex.conversation_starts), across CLI and desktop.

panels.title: Token Usage Over Time — by Type
panels.description: Hourly input, output, cached, and reasoning token totals. Watch for rising input/cached over a long session (context growth).

panels.title: Tokens by Surface (CLI vs Desktop)
panels.description: Share of output tokens generated by the Codex CLI (codex_exec) versus the desktop app (codex-app-server).

targets.expr: sum by (service_name) (sum_over_time({service_name=~"codex-app-server|codex_exec"} | json | attributes_event_name="codex.sse_event" | unwrap attributes_output_token_count [$__range]))

panels.title: Total Tool Calls
panels.description: Count of tool executions (codex.tool_result) across both surfaces.

panels.title: Tool Success Rate %
panels.description: Percentage of tool_result events with success="true". Below 70% turns red, 70–85% yellow, above 85% green.

panels.title: p95 Turn Latency (ms)
panels.description: 95th percentile WebSocket round-trip duration (codex.websocket_event duration_ms) — the closest available signal to model response time. Green < 5s, yellow < 15s, red ≥ 15s.

panels.title: Turns Over Time
panels.description: Completed model responses (sse_event response.completed) per hour. A proxy for request volume.

panels.title: Event Volume by Type — Over Time
panels.description: Codex event counts per hour by event type, excluding the high-volume websocket_event noise. Shows the rhythm of conversations, prompts, tool calls, and completions.

panels.title: Git Commands
panels.description: Count of shell tool calls whose command text contains 'git ' — a proxy for git activity. Codex emits no native git metrics, so this is derived from the command stream (codex.tool_result arguments).

panels.title: Commits
panels.description: Count of shell tool calls containing 'git commit' — a proxy for commits made by Codex. Derived from command text, not a native counter (Codex does not emit one). Pairs with Claude Code's Commits panel.

panels.title: File Edit Actions
panels.description: Shell tool calls that write or patch files (Set-Content, Out-File, apply_patch, Add-Content, or shell redirection into a file) — a proxy for code edits. Codex routes file edits through the shell, so this is command-derived, not a line count.

panels.title: Code Activity Over Time (proxy)
panels.description: Hourly count of git commands and file-edit actions detected in Codex's shell command stream. A proxy for code-modification activity since Codex emits no native lines-of-code or commit metrics. Compare shape to Claude Code's Code Modification Velocity.

panels.title: Prompts Per Hour
panels.description: User prompts submitted per hour (codex.user_prompt). Peaks show concentrated interaction.

panels.title: Tool Usage Breakdown
panels.description: Share of tool calls by tool name (codex.tool_result). shell_command dominating is typical for coding work.

panels.title: Tool Decision Outcomes
panels.description: How tool executions were authorized (codex.tool_decision): approved / denied / ask. A high denied/ask share means Codex is hitting approval friction.

panels.title: Decision Source
panels.description: What authorized each tool decision (codex.tool_decision source): Config (approval policy), or interactive user approval.

panels.title: Tool Calls Over Time — by Tool
panels.description: Tool call volume per hour, split by tool name. Bursts of shell_command track active build/test cycles.

panels.title: MCP Server Attribution — Tool Calls
panels.description: Tool calls attributed to an MCP server (codex.tool_result mcp_server, non-empty). Built-in tools (shell, file) have no mcp_server and are excluded.

panels.title: Most Used Tools
panels.description: Per-tool breakdown over the selected range: uses, average duration, and success rate. Sourced from codex.tool_result, joined on tool_name.

targets.expr: sum by(attributes_tool_name)(count_over_time({service_name=~"codex-app-server|codex_exec"} | json | attributes_event_name="codex.tool_result" | attributes_success="true" [$__range])) / sum by(attributes_tool_name)(count_over_time({service_name=~"codex-app-server|codex_exec"} | json | attributes_event_name="codex.tool_result" [$__range])) * 100

panels.title: Tool Success Rate by Tool
panels.description: Success rate (success="true") per tool over the selected range. Surfaces which specific tools are failing. Red below 70%, yellow to 85%, green above.

targets.expr: sum by(attributes_tool_name)(count_over_time({service_name=~"codex-app-server|codex_exec"} | json | attributes_event_name="codex.tool_result" | attributes_success="true" [$__range])) / sum by(attributes_tool_name)(count_over_time({service_name=~"codex-app-server|codex_exec"} | json | attributes_event_name="codex.tool_result" [$__range])) * 100

panels.title: p95 Turn Latency by Model (ms)
panels.description: 95th percentile WebSocket round-trip (websocket_event duration_ms) grouped by model. Codex uses WebSocket transport, so this is the available latency signal. Yellow at 5s, red at 15s.

panels.title: Avg Turn Latency Over Time — by Model
panels.description: Average WebSocket round-trip duration per hour by model. Rising latency can indicate larger context or server backpressure.

panels.title: Transport Errors
panels.description: Count of WebSocket events with success="false" — failed transport round-trips. Green at zero; any failures turn it yellow, 5+ red.

panels.title: WebSocket Round-Trip Rate (events/min)
panels.description: WebSocket event throughput per minute — a measure of how chatty Codex's transport is. Spikes track active generation.

panels.title: Avg TTFT (ms)
panels.description: Average time to first token (codex.turn_ttft duration_ms) over the selected range. Green < 5s, yellow < 15s, red above.

panels.title: p95 API Latency (TTFT)
panels.description: Average API request round-trip (codex.api_request duration_ms, real model requests) over the selected range. Green < 30s, yellow < 90s, red above.

panels.title: p95 API Latency by Model (TTFT)
panels.description: 95th percentile API request round-trip duration by model (codex.api_request). This is the full HTTP round-trip to OpenAI's Responses endpoint, complementary to the WebSocket transport latency above.

targets.expr: quantile_over_time(0.95, {service_name=~"codex-app-server|codex_exec"} | json | attributes_event_name="codex.turn_ttft" | __error__="" | unwrap attributes_duration_ms [$__range]) by (attributes_model)

panels.title: p95 API Latency Over Time — by Model (TTFT)
panels.description: Average API request round-trip per hour by model (codex.api_request). Rising latency can indicate larger context or server-side slowdowns.

targets.expr: quantile_over_time(0.95, {service_name=~"codex-app-server|codex_exec"} | json | attributes_event_name="codex.turn_ttft" | __error__="" | unwrap attributes_duration_ms [$__auto]) by (attributes_model)

panels.title: TTFT Over Time — by Model
panels.description: Average time to first token per hour by model (codex.turn_ttft). How quickly the model starts responding.

panels.title: API Request Errors — HTTP Status
panels.description: Count of non-200 API responses by HTTP status code (codex.api_request). Empty is healthy.

panels.title: API Requests (Responses)
panels.description: Count of real API requests over the selected range (codex.api_request with a model set, excludes /models housekeeping calls).

panels.title: Trace: p95 Operation Latency by Span
panels.description: 95th percentile latency per Codex operation, derived from trace spans (Tempo span metrics in Prometheus). Shows where time goes inside a turn — model sampling, websocket round-trips, tool discovery, etc. Sourced from codex_exec and the desktop app's traces. Populates under sustained usage; a single one-off turn may not have enough samples for a percentile.

panels.title: Trace: Avg Operation Latency by Span
panels.description: Average latency per Codex operation from trace span metrics (sum/count). Complements the p95 view and is available even from a single turn.

panels.title: Trace: Operation Call Rate by Span
panels.description: Per-operation call rate from trace span metrics (traces_spanmetrics_calls_total). Shows which Codex operations run most and when.

panels.title: Trace: End-to-End Turn Duration
panels.description: Average duration of the run_turn span over time — the full end-to-end time for one Codex conversation turn, derived from traces. Complements TTFT (first-token): this is the total turn time. Works from CLI traces, no desktop dependency.

panels.title: Conversations by Model
panels.description: Distribution of conversations by model (codex.conversation_starts model).

panels.title: Conversations by Approval Policy
panels.description: Split of conversations by approval_policy (never / on-request / on-failure / untrusted).

panels.title: Conversations by Surface
panels.description: CLI (codex_exec) vs desktop app (codex-app-server) conversation share.

panels.title: Conversations by Reasoning Effort
panels.description: Distribution of conversations by reasoning_effort (low / medium / high / xhigh). A heavy xhigh share drives up reasoning-token usage.

panels.title: New Conversations Over Time
panels.description: New Codex conversations started per hour across both surfaces.

panels.title: Conversations by Originator
panels.description: What launched each conversation (codex.conversation_starts originator). 'Claude_Code' means Claude Code spawned Codex; 'codex_exec' is the CLI used directly. Useful for spotting agent-to-agent orchestration.

panels.title: Avg Session Metrics
panels.description: Average tool calls per conversation (total tool_result events divided by distinct conversations that used tools) and the count of those conversations, over the selected range.

panels.title: Codex Event Stream
panels.description: Raw Codex log stream (both surfaces), excluding high-volume websocket noise. Click a line to expand the full structured payload.
